[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network – v1.1.2 Release Now Live**

The latest Android release of the Unity Network application is now available.

Version 1.1.2 introduces new marketplace functionality, real-time notifications, and the first opt-in verification task.

Download
• Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.unitynodes.unityapp
• Direct APK: https://releases.unitynodes.io/android/

What's new in v1.1.2
• Marketplace Enhancements - Filters added, listings now randomized with daily reshuffling
• License & Lease Details - Additional information included on listings
• Notification Framework - Users will now be notified of important events in near real-time
• Opt-in CLI Verification Task - Validate Caller Line Identification integrity by receiving inbound calls and reporting CLI data for route verification
• UI/UX improvements and additional housekeeping

No uninstall is required - install directly over your current version.

This release marks an important step forward, introducing the first opt-in task to the network. Unity Network continues to expand as a globally distributed, people-powered edge network.

Join the distributed edge network. Secure your Unity Node and download the Unity Network App at unitynodes.io

author: Joshua F
msg:
Hi all, I want to share a thought based on my experience onboarding new ULOs (especially non-technical ones) to simplify the UX. What if the default in-app flow had no marketplace and just used simple standard rules:

 • All licences are 60% UNO / 40% ULO and if no referral is used one is selected at random from all available (no marketplace).

 • Each UNO has one referral code. If a new user enters it, they claim a license and get +10% rewards uplift  (50% UNO / 50% ULO)

 • All lease lengths are infinite as long as 80% uptime is maintained; UNO can only revoke if uptime drops below that.

 • UNOs can still set any UNO/ULO split which can be claimed manually with the longer codes we use today.

Why: avoids race-to-the-bottom, massively simpler for non-technical users (don't need to understand UNO ULO or marketplace - just that they earn 10% more rewards by using a code), easier for UNOs to onboard beyond our immediate circles, strong incentive for UNOs to start marketing.

author: Byron
msg:
Hello people. I have read all messages regarding the marketplace and the minimum share. Let me give you my two cents. I believe its wrong for us UNOs to argue regarding what is going to happen to the marketplace. Simply because, at up until now, at least, we do not have any kind of actual useful data to work with. The team knows and configures everything. The supply (tasks) but also they have the full dataset of the demand (leases, type of devices, locations - so can easily correlate leases with average wage etc).

All it takes for the team is a couple of smart marketeers to dive into the data and regulate the market so as to achieve the best possible  performance. I believe they should run some models and decide on an algorithm which will update the UNO/ULO dynamically based on market structure/KPIs (e.g. percent of leased/unleased licenses, type of devices (wifi only, sim enabled etc), farms or physical devices, ULO locations and of course type and number of tasks available at each time). The goal of the model would be to maximize revenue for both UNOs and ULOs alike of course.

So the marketplace, for me, should be a regulated market. A dynamic UNO/ULO split should be applied for any new license a ULO is taking from the marketplace. UNOs will not be able to change that for their licenses available on the marketplace. Of course we will be free to take our licenses off the marketplace and apply whatever split we like for our own ULOs.

An unregulated market will still find equilibrium but in our case specifically will certainly take way more time to do so (due to the lack of information to the participants) compared to a fully regulated one.
